description: POST /v1/automations/:id/run — role: viewer/owner. Increments the automation's monotonic run_request_id and returns 200 with NO body. NOT a command and NOT a state write: mints no config.event, does not advance space_revision, enqueues nothing in CommandStore (v1-architecture.md §5.1, P0-4). The resident agent runs it on the next GET /v1/automations poll when run_request_id advances. Matches openapi.yaml #/paths/~1v1~1automations~1{id}~1run/post.

response.note: No response body — run_request_id was incremented (here 4 -> 5). The optional Idempotency-Key dedups a network retry of THIS tap: replaying the same key returns 200 without a second increment.

effect_visible_in_get_automations.description: Immediately after the run POST, GET /v1/automations shows the automation with the advanced run_request_id. The resident agent (which polls this route as its heartbeat) runs the automation because run_request_id (5) is strictly greater than its last-consumed in-memory value (4), then records 5. run_requested_at is display-only.

error_response_404_unknown_automation.note: Existence is checked before writing, same discipline as PATCH/DELETE — incrementing run_request_id on a nonexistent automation is a 404, not a silent no-op.

notes: Caller is the viewer device from join.json — 'run' is one of the automation routes viewers may call (v1-architecture.md §3), reflecting pairing-and-control.md's 'the phone may also run, pause, reschedule or delete an existing automation'.

This is NOT a command: it does not go through CommandStore, WS command frames, or the POST /v1/events commands[] piggyback — those carry only task-scoped pause/resume/stop. run_now no longer exists in the command channel (v1-architecture.md §1.4, §5.1). This fix exists precisely because the resident agent never opens a WS, so a WS-only run_now command would never be claimed.

Exactly-once via a monotonic id, not a wall-clock (P0-4): the agent keys off run_request_id compared against fresh in-memory last-consumed state, so a short automation finishing between the 2s poll and the 10s config refresh cannot re-fire the same id. run_requested_at is display-only; the agent never keys off it. An optional Idempotency-Key makes a network retry of the same tap not double-increment; without it a retry just advances the counter and the agent still runs at most once per distinct id it observes.
